---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 96b44f049d11bfef37e017d5e8f7dcb462a58ef4
findings:
  - { id: judged-dcf-1, kind: judged, text: "derivation-drawer dc-2 adds a judged-findings case-file chip sourced from align:judged-sweep, but parent wall-receipts dc-1 fixes the v1 badge set as a closed enumeration of existing computes (VL findings, spec-stale, pending-supersession, empty evidence slots, size-smell) — the judged-sweep chip is a new wall surface outside that list, and dc-2's prose citation of dc-1's no-invented-computation clause is not a declared supersedes/exempts edge against the enumerated badge set (confidence 0.65)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "wall-receipts ac-6 — the same frozen spec as dc-1, and the AC this story's stub is assigned — requires judged findings on the wall displaying their sweep provenance, so the surface is the parent's own mandate, not this story's extension; dc-1's enumeration closes the set of COMPUTES whose outputs badge ('existing computes only ... no computation is invented'), and the judged sweep is an existing compute (verdi align's design-branch sweep) whose already-written report this chip reads — a closed compute list cannot be read to forbid what the same spec's ac-6 mandates, and no computation is invented", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }
  - { id: judged-dcf-2, kind: judged, text: "derivation-drawer dc-3 introduces a new deterministic computation (comparing the report's covers against the current spec content digest and decisions_scanned against currently-declared decision ids) that is not among the existing computes wall-receipts dc-1 restricts v1 to; dc-3 sidesteps by rendering disclosure lines rather than a badge, but no exempts edge declares that the drawer body is out of dc-1's no-invented-computation scope (confidence 0.40)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "the drawer renders two operands that both already exist as pinned inputs — the report's own covers/decisions_scanned fields and the current spec digest and declared decision ids the wall already holds for every derivation record — and the equality that decides whether a mismatch line renders is presentation over those present operands: it produces no new verdict, no badge, and no stored value, which is what dc-1's no-invented-computation clause protects; ac-6's 'so a stale or partial sweep looks stale' requires exactly this legibility, and rendering the operands without the contrast would fail the parent's AC", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }
  - { id: judged-dcf-3, kind: judged, text: "derivation-drawer dc-3 declares staleness is 'comparison, not verdict' and never a computed stale verdict badge, while parent wall-receipts dc-1 ships a spec-stale verdict badge in the same v1 wall — the two staleness postures coexist on one surface without an edge declaring why the report-staleness disclosure model does not supersede or conflict with the spec-stale badge model (confidence 0.30)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "the two postures answer different questions over disjoint operands and never collide: spec-stale is an existing ladder compute over deviation-report dispositions (a badge of the v1 set, with its own derivation record), while dc-3's contrast is a receipt-reading aid inside a drawer about the judged sweep's own inputs — neither reads the other's operands, produces the other's output shape, nor gates anything; their coexistence on one wall is the parent's own design (dc-1 ships spec-stale, ac-6 ships sweep-provenance legibility), so there is nothing to supersede", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/derivation-drawer#dc-1, spec/derivation-drawer#dc-2, spec/derivation-drawer#dc-3, spec/derivation-drawer#dc-4, spec/wall-receipts#dc-1, spec/wall-receipts#dc-2, spec/wall-receipts#dc-3, spec/wall-receipts#dc-4] }
digest: sha256:2972c86a2e1d59d9fee0983fc9893b7a9082f518b2291b1a35e7d3e41d324658
integrity: sha256:775c2a57cd30104b5fc15034a50e77777e1e59a34fd69c4963b26cfc67c3704e
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"dcf-1\",\"text\":\"derivation-drawer dc-2 adds a judged-findings case-file chip sourced from align:judged-sweep, but parent wall-receipts dc-1 fixes the v1 badge set as a closed enumeration of existing computes (VL findings, spec-stale, pending-supersession, empty evidence slots, size-smell) — the judged-sweep chip is a new wall surface outside that list, and dc-2's prose citation of dc-1's no-invented-computation clause is not a declared supersedes/exempts edge against the enumerated badge set\",\"confidence\":0.65,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"},{\"id\":\"dcf-2\",\"text\":\"derivation-drawer dc-3 introduces a new deterministic computation (comparing the report's covers against the current spec content digest and decisions_scanned against currently-declared decision ids) that is not among the existing computes wall-receipts dc-1 restricts v1 to; dc-3 sidesteps by rendering disclosure lines rather than a badge, but no exempts edge declares that the drawer body is out of dc-1's no-invented-computation scope\",\"confidence\":0.4,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"},{\"id\":\"dcf-3\",\"text\":\"derivation-drawer dc-3 declares staleness is 'comparison, not verdict' and never a computed stale verdict badge, while parent wall-receipts dc-1 ships a spec-stale verdict badge in the same v1 wall — the two staleness postures coexist on one surface without an edge declaring why the report-staleness disclosure model does not supersede or conflict with the spec-stale badge model\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dcf-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: derivation-drawer dc-2 adds a judged-findings case-file chip sourced from align:judged-sweep, but parent wall-receipts dc-1 fixes the v1 badge set as a closed enumeration of existing computes (VL findings, spec-stale, pending-supersession, empty evidence slots, size-smell) — the judged-sweep chip is a new wall surface outside that list, and dc-2's prose citation of dc-1's no-invented-computation clause is not a declared supersedes/exempts edge against the enumerated badge set (confidence 0.65)
- **judged-dcf-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: derivation-drawer dc-3 introduces a new deterministic computation (comparing the report's covers against the current spec content digest and decisions_scanned against currently-declared decision ids) that is not among the existing computes wall-receipts dc-1 restricts v1 to; dc-3 sidesteps by rendering disclosure lines rather than a badge, but no exempts edge declares that the drawer body is out of dc-1's no-invented-computation scope (confidence 0.40)
- **judged-dcf-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: derivation-drawer dc-3 declares staleness is 'comparison, not verdict' and never a computed stale verdict badge, while parent wall-receipts dc-1 ships a spec-stale verdict badge in the same v1 wall — the two staleness postures coexist on one surface without an edge declaring why the report-staleness disclosure model does not supersede or conflict with the spec-stale badge model (confidence 0.30)
